naga_aspirant.name: Naga Aspirant
description: ## Naga Aspirant
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/196›

The naga aspirant follows the ancient beliefs and engages in the rituals of a druidic sect dedicated to the transcendence of her nagaji form through absolute devotion to nagas and naga gods. Through acting as a herald to the naga deities, the aspirant is rewarded with the ability to unlock her ultimate spirit form and become a true naga. A naga aspirant has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Nagaji}

::ab[Aspirant's Bond (Ex)]{icon=learn flavor="A naga aspirant gains a spiritual connection to the serpentine deities worshiped by the nagas." passive="At 1st level, and @HLeach_time_she_gains_a_druid_level, she may add one of the following spells to her druid spell list." s0="Acid splash~~bleed~~daze~~mage hand~~open/close~~ray of frost" s1="Charm person~~divine favor~~expeditious retreat~~mage armor~~magic missile~~ray of enfeeblement~~shield~~shield of faith~~silent image~~true strike" s2="Acid arrow~~detect thoughts~~invisibility~~mirror image~~scorching ray~~see invisibility" s3="Dispel magic~~displacement~~fireball~~lightning bolt~~suggestion" s4="Divine power~~greater invisibility" replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Aspirant's Enlightenment (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A naga aspirant gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like abilities, supernatural abilities, and poison of ‹family/naga«s›." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Naga Shape (Su)]{icon=boost l4="The naga aspirant gains the *wild shape* ability, as a normal druid." l6="The naga aspirant can use her *wild shape* ability to assume the form of a true naga. This effect functions in a similar manner to a ‹spell/shapechange› spell with the following exception. The druid's true naga form is unique, representing her personal evolution. When taking naga form, the nagaji's body transforms into that of a large serpent, though she keeps her own head. The naga aspirant loses her limbs and her size increases by one category, granting her a +4 size bonus to Strength and Constitution, a -2 penalty to Dexterity, and a +2 enhancement bonus to her natural armor bonus. She gains a +10 enhancement bonus to land speed and a ‹umr/bite attack› that deals 1d6 points of damage. She can cast verbal spells in this form, but cannot cast spells with other components without metamagic or feats such as ‹feat/Natural Spell›. This otherwise works like and replaces *wild shape.*" replace="Wild Shape"}

::ab[Augmented Form (Su)]{head icon=power l9="A naga aspirant can choose one of the following abilities to enhance her naga form. Once chosen, this augmentation cannot be changed and always applies to her naga form. The caster level for these abilities is equal to her druid level, and unless otherwise stated, the DC is equal to 10 + ‹misc/half› the druid's class level + the druid's Charisma bonus." l13="The aspirant gains another ability from the list below." l17="The aspirant gains a third ability from the list below." replace="Venom immunity~a thousand faces~timeless body"}
::ab[Charming Gaze (Sp)]{next icon=zap ability="The druid gains a ‹umr/gaze› attack that affects creatures within 30 feet as a ‹spell/charm person› spell."}
::ab[Darkvision (Su)]{next icon=power passive="The druid gains darkvision with a range of 60 feet."}
::ab[Detect Thoughts (Su)]{next icon=magic ability="The druid can use *detect thoughts* at will."}
::ab[Guarded Thoughts (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="The druid gains a +2 racial bonus on saves against charm effects and immunity to any form of mind reading (such as *detect thoughts*)."}
::ab[Poison Immunity (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="The druid gains immunity to all poisons." prereq="The druid's naga form must have at least one poison-based natural attack."}
:::ab{next title="Poisonous Sting (Ex)" icon=melee action="Passive Ability" prereq="The naga form's tail must have a *sting.*"}
The druid's stinger becomes venomous.
::aff{iconP type=Sting-injury dcF dcLev=druid dcMod=Con dcIt=her freq="1 round" eff="sleep for 2d4 minutes" cure1}

::ab[Spit Venom (Ex)]{next icon=zap standard="The naga form can spit her venom up to 30 feet. This is a ranged touch attack with no range increment. Any opponent hit by this attack must make a successful save (see Venemous Bite poison below) to avoid the effect." prereq="The naga form must have a *venomous bite.*"}
::ab[Sting (Ex)]{next icon=melee passive="The naga form grows a stinger on the end of her tail, granting her a sting natural attack that deals 1d6 points of piercing damage."}
::ab[Swim (Ex)]{next icon=power passive="The naga form gains a swim speed equal to her base speed."}
::ab[Tough Scales (Ex)]{next icon=def passive="The druid's enhancement bonus to her natural armor increases by +2." special="The druid may select this ability more than once. Its effects stack."}
:::ab{next title="Venomous Bite (Ex)" icon=melee action="Passive Ability"}
The naga form's bite attack becomes poisonous.
::aff{iconP type=Bite-injury dcF dcLev=druid dcMod=Con freqR=6 effCon=1 cure1 dcIt=her}

::ab[True Naga (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="A naga aspirant metamorphoses into a unique naga. Her *wild shape* form becomes her natural form, though she can transform into her original nagaji shape at will. Her creature type permanently changes to aberration." replace="Wildshape (at will)"}

nature_fang.name: Nature Fang
description: ## Nature Fang
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/92›

A nature fang is a druid who stalks and slays those who despoil nature, kill scarce animals, or introduce diseases to unprotected habitats. She gives up a close empathic connection with the natural world to become its deadly champion and avenger.

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A nature fang gains the ‹class/slayer«'s› *studied target* class feature. Unlike a slayer, a nature fang does not gain the ability to maintain more than one studied target at the same time." replace="Nature sense~wild empathy~woodland stride" head}

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{next flavor="Text from the slayer class" icon=boost move="You can study an opponent you can see. You then gain a bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks attempted against that opponent, and a bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against it; these bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of your slayer level. The DCs of slayer class abilities against that opponent increase by 1 + one-fifth of your slayer level. These bonuses remain in effect until either the opponent is dead or you study a new target." order=mif immediate="If you deal sneak attack damage to a target, you can study that target immediately, allowing you to apply your studied target bonuses against that target (including to the normal weapon damage roll)." free="You can discard this connection to a studied target, allowing you to study another target in its place."}

::ab[Slayer Talent]{l=4 icon=power choice="A nature fang selects a ‹ability/slayer talent<s›. At every even-numbered level (6th, 8th, 10th, and so on), she gains a new slayer talent. She uses her druid level as her slayer level to determine what talents she can select." imp12="She can select an ‹ability/advanced slayer talent<s› in place of a slayer talent." special="The following slayer talents complement the nature fang archetype: ‹slayertalent/foil scrutiny›, ‹slayertalent/poison use›, ‹slayertalent/ranger combat style›. Additionally, the following ‹ability/rogue talent«s›, which are also available to slayers, complement the archetype: ‹talent/bleeding attack›, ‹talent/camouflage›, ‹talent/fast stealth›, ‹talent/finesse rogue›, ‹talent/lasting poison›, ‹talent/rogue crawl›, ‹talent/slow reactions›, ‹talent/surprise attack›, ‹talent/swift poison›, ‹talent/terrain mastery›. The following advanced talents complement the nature fang archetype: ‹slayertalent/assassinate›, ‹slayertalent/slayer camouflage›, ‹slayertalent/woodland stride›. The advanced rogue talent ‹talent/master of disguise› also fits the archetype." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Sneak Attack (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A nature fang gains ‹ability/sneak attack› +1d6. This functions as the ‹class/rogue› sneak attack ability. If the nature fang gets a sneak attack bonus from another source, the bonuses on damage stack." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Swift Studied Target]{l=9 icon=boost ability="A nature fang can study an opponent as a @HLmove or @HLswift_action." replace="Venom immunity"}

nature_priest.name: Nature Priest
description: ## Nature Priest
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/23›
Nature priests serve two masters: their deities and nature itself, and can call upon the power of both to fuel their divine abilities.
::prereq{other="Like a cleric, a nature priest must worship a deity and her alignment must be within one step of her deity's."}
::cskill{gain=kr lose=kg}
::ab[Weapon Proficiencies]{icon=power note="A nature priest is proficient with her deity's favored weapon in addition to a druid's typical weapon proficiencies." alter="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Studious Piety (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A nature priest gains a +1 bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks. This bonus increases to +2 when attempting checks that involve knowledge of her deity or her faith." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Chosen Druid (Ex)]{icon=info choice="If a nature priest chooses a domain for her *nature bond,* she must choose one of her deity's domains (or subdomains) in place of those typically available to a druid." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Shepherd of the Faithful (Su)]{l=4 icon=def-aura passive="A nature priest and all allies within 60 feet that can see or hear her gain a +2 insight bonus on skill checks to notice or avoid natural hazards, difficult terrain, and rough winds or water, as well as on saving throws against natural hazards. This ability offers no protection against the abilities of creatures or the effects of spells or items." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Divine Servants (Su)]{l=9 icon=learn-boost passive="A nature priest adds the spells ‹spell/lesser planar ally›, ‹spell/planar ally›, and ‹spell/greater planar ally› to her druid spell list at their cleric spell levels. When she casts these spells, the planar allies summoned require half the payment described in those spells." replace="Venom immunity"}

nithveil_adept.name: Nithveil Adept
description: ## Nithveil Adept
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/44›

Nithveil adepts learn the secret magic of the First World fey in the moving, reality-phasing city of Nithveil, which appears on the new moon at seemingly random locations in the Grungir Forest in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The most common deities worshiped by these druids are the Green Mother and the Lantern King, Eldest patrons of intrigue and trickery.
::cskill{gain=bluff~diplo~dis~sm~perf~stl post="This also replaces the Nature Sense ability"}

::ab[Nature Bond (Su)]{icon=down-boost choice="A Nithveil adept cannot select an animal companion, and can take only a domain. If she worships an ‹rule/Eldest›, the Nithveil trickster can choose any of the domains or subdomains granted by that Eldest in addition to those normally available to druids." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Animal Speech (Su)]{icon=magic flavor="A Nithveil adept can imbue normal animals with the ability to speak for a brief period." standard="The Nithveil adept can touch an animal to grant it the ability to speak any language the druid knows for 1 minute per druid level. This does not increase the animal's Intelligence or improve its attitude, and the animal behaves as though communicating with someone under the effects of a ‹spell/speak with animals› spell, save that it can speak with anyone who understands the language the Nithveil adept grants it." usage="A number of times per day equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of her druid level." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Resist Fey Influence (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def-roll order=pa passive="A Nithveil adept gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of fey." ability="Once per day before attempting such a saving throw, the Nithveil adept can choose to roll two dice and take the better result, applying the +4 bonus as normal." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Locate Nithveil (Sp)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="A Nithveil adept can cast ‹spell/find the path› as a spell-like ability, but only to locate Nithveil. The spell's duration is extended until the druid reaches her destination or until Nithveil recedes from the Material Plane at the end of the new moon." usage="This ability can be used only once per month, during the new moon."}

pack_lord.name: Pack Lord
description: ## Pack Lord
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/38›
Some druids bond with many animal companions rather than just one, achieving a level of communion rare even in druidic circles and leading their pack brothers and pack sisters with total authority.

:::ab{icon=down action=Special choice="A pack lord druid may not select a domain and must choose an animal companion." passive="The druid gains a +2 bonus on *wild empathy* and Handle Animal checks made regarding her animal companion." title="Pack Bond (Ex)" replace="Nature bond" order=cpC}

The pack lord may have more than one animal companion, but she must divide up her effective druid level between her companions to determine the abilities of each companion. For example, a 4th-level pack lord can have one 4th-level companion, two 2nd-level companions, or one 1st-level and one 3rd-level companion.

Each time a pack lord's druid level increases, she must decide how to allocate the increase among her animal companions (including the option of adding a new 1st-level companion). Once a druid level is allocated to a particular companion, it cannot be redistributed while that companion is in the pack lord's service (she must release the companion or wait until the companion dies to allocate its levels to another companion, which she can do the next time she prepares spells). The *share spells* animal companion ability only applies to one animal companion at a time - the pack lord cannot use it to cast a one-target spell and have it affect all of her animal companions.

::ab[Improved Empathic Link (Su)]{icon=power ability="The pack lord gains an *empathic link* with all of her animal companions. This functions like an empathic link with a ‹sidekick/familiar›." order=aw swift="The pack lord can shift her perception to one of her companions, allowing her to experience what it sees, hears, and so on. She can maintain this connection as long as she likes (as long as the companion is within 1 mile) and end it as a @HLfree_action. The druid can only use this ability on one companion at a time, and cannot see, hear, or smell with her own body while maintaining this connection." replace="The 6th-level additional use of *wild shape*"}

plains_druid.name: Plains Druid
description: ## Plains Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/100›

Out upon the wide and rolling prairies and savannahs, plains druids stand guard over the grasslands. These druids range far and wide, watching over nomadic tribes and wandering herds and preserving the sometimes fragile ecosystem of the wide open spaces.

::ab[Plains Traveler (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A plains druid gains a bonus on initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in plains terrain, and she cannot be tracked in such an environment." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Run Like the Wind (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-power order=pa passive="A plains druid gains +10 feet to her land speed when wearing light or no armor and carrying a light load." ability="Once per hour, she may ‹rule/run› or ‹rule/charge› at double the normal speed for 1 round." special="If riding her animal companion, it gains this ability instead." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Savanna Ambush (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="A plains druid gains ‹rule/concealment› whenever she is ‹rule/prone› in natural surroundings, and can make Stealth checks at no penalty when prone and not moving or at -5 when crawling." order=pi immediate="A plains druid can stand up from prone during a surprise round." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{icon=down l=6 ability="A plains druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Canny Charger (Ex)]{l=9 icon=power-def-boost ability="A plains druid can charge through allies' squares without difficulty (whether mounted or afoot) and can turn up to 90 degrees once during a charge, provided the last 10 feet toward the target are in a straight line." order=ap passive="She also gains a +4 dodge bonus to AC against enemy charge attacks and a +4 bonus to damage with a ‹rule/readied action› against a charging foe." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="A plains druid gains ‹ability/evasion› when wearing light or no armor and carrying a light load. This functions as the ‹class/rogue› ability of the same name." replace="A thousand faces"}

planar_extremist.name: Planar Extremist
description: ## Planar Extremist
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/13›

Through determined interest or repeated exposure to those places where the borders between planes are weaker, some druids lose their neutral stance and find themselves gravitating toward one of the four most extreme alignments: chaotic evil, chaotic good, lawful evil, or lawful good. While no longer able to harness the forces of nature like their neutral kin, these planar extremists find that their powers have shifted to reflect the Outer Plane most closely associated with their alignment. This is an ex-class archetype and can be taken by a character immediately upon becoming an ex-druid.

@HL[The following archetype can be taken by an ex-druid immediately upon becoming an ex-druid, regardless of character level, replacing some or all of the lost class abilities.] If another archetype the character had before she became an ex-druid replaces the same ability as the ex-class archetype, she loses the old archetype in favor of the new one; otherwise, she can retain both archetypes as normal. Planar extremixts can gain further levels in the druid class, even though becoming an ex-druid normally prohibits further advancement in the class. While an ex-member of a class can recant her failings and atone for her fall from her original class (typically involving an ‹spell/atonement› spell), her acceptance of her ex-class archetype means she must atone both for her initial fall and for further straying from the path. As a result, such a character must be the target of two atonement spells or a similar effect to regain her lost class features. Upon doing so, she immediately loses this archetype and regains her original class (and archetype, if she had one).
::prereq{other="Must have an alignment of chaotic evil, chaotic good, lawful evil, or lawful good."}
::ab[Aura (Ex)]{icon=aura passive="The planar extremist radiates an aura matching her alignment as if she were a ‹class/cleric› of her druid level."}

::ab[Spells]{icon=down ability="A planar extremist gains one fewer spell slot per spell level than normal in which to prepare spells. The planar extremist removes all *summon nature's ally* spells from her spell list and replaces them with the ‹spell/summon monster› spells of the same levels. The druid can otherwise cast spells as normal for a druid of her level." alter=Spells}

::ab[Planar Bond]{icon=power head choice="A planar extremist forms a bond with a manifestation of the Outer Plane with which she is aligned. This bond can take one of two forms." replace="Nature's bond"}

::ab[Domain Option]{sub icon=magic ability="The first is a close tie to the plane to which she is aligned, granting the planar extremist one of the domains of her alignment (for example, a lawful good planar extremist could take either the ‹domain/Law› or ‹domain/Good› domain). This option otherwise functions as a druid's *nature bond* if she chose a close tie to the natural world."}
:::ab{sub title="Outsider Option" action=Ability icon=power}

The second option is to form a close bond with an outsider from an Outer Plane. The abilities of this outsider companion are determined using the rules for ‹sidekick/unchained_>eidolon«s› for the ‹class/unchained summoner› class, as if the planar extremist were a summoner of her druid level, except the outsider companion gains no additional evolution pool (only the evolutions from its base form and base evolutions for its subtype), and it must be of a ‹ability/unchained_eidolon_>subtype<s› whose alignment exactly matches the alignment of the planar extremist. The planar extremist can summon her outsider companion with the same 1-minute ritual a summoner normally uses to do so, but she can't cast *summon monster* spells if she currently has her outsider companion summoned, and she can't summon her companion if she already has a creature summoned through other means.

As the planar ally gains class levels, her eidolon's base statistics and base evolutions increase as if her druid level were her summoner level. The eidolon gains the *darkvision, link, share spells, evasion, ability score increase, devotion, multiattack,* and *improved evasion* abilities at the appropriate levels, but never gains an evolution pool. Abilities and spells that grant additional evolution points to eidolons do not function for her outsider companion, though any abilities that would grant evolution points to an animal companion do work. The planar extremist does not gain life link or any other class features a summoner gains in relation to her eidolon.

::ab[Spontaneous Casting]{icon=magic ability="A planar extremist can channel stored spell energy into summoning spells that she hasn't prepared ahead of time. She can lose a prepared spell in order to cast a *summon monster* spell of the same level or lower, but only to summon creatures whose alignment matches hers." alter="A druid's spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Resist the Opposite (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A planar extremist gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of creatures whose alignment is diametrically opposed to her own." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Planar Aspect (Su)]{l=4 icon=power standard="Once per day, a planar extremist can gain the benefits of the bloodrager bloodline associated with her alignment (choosing from ‹blrgbloodline/Abyssal›, ‹blrgbloodline/Celestial›, or ‹blrgbloodline/Infernal›), as if she were a ‹class/bloodrager› of her druid level. She can gain these benefits for 1 minute per druid level as if she were bloodraging (but she gains no other benefits or penalties of bloodrage) or until she dismisses it as a @HLswift_action; this duration need not be used consecutively but must be spent in 1-minute increments." imp6="She can use this ability twice a day." imp10="She can use this three times a day." imp14="She can use this four times a day." imp18="She can use this five times a day." imp20="A planar extremist can use *planar aspect* at will." replace="Wild shape"}

progenitor.name: Progenitor
description: ## Progenitor
‹SOURCE Planar Adventures/19›

Some druids forge powerful bonds with the raw, natural power of the First World. Progenitors are druids who tap into the vibrant power of the First World to propagate natural and supernatural wonders.

::ab[Infused Summoning (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="When a progenitor casts *summon nature's ally* to summon a creature, the summoned creature gains ‹umr/fast healing› equal to the progenitor's level (maximum equal to the progenitor's Wisdom modifier)." alter="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Primal Bond (Ex)]{head icon=magic choice="A progenitor infuses herself with fey magic so that she is treated as either her original creature type or the fey type for the purposes of spells or effects that target a creature by type, whichever is more beneficial to her. Additionally, the progenitor chooses one of two forms through which this bond expresses itself: magic or nature." replace="Nature bond"}

::ab[Magic]{sub icon=magic note="A progenitor who chooses magic as her primal bond can draw upon wild magic to attempt to cast a prepared spell without losing that spell from its spell slot." order=nwu swift="To use this ability, she casts the spell as she would normally, but as the spell's effects take place, she must use a @HLswift_action to attempt a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC = 20 + twice the spell's level). If she fails, she expends the spell normally as if she had cast it, but its effects are replaced by ‹rule/a wild »magic «effect<traits_pa› with a CR equal to her caster level and she is ‹misc/staggered› until the end of her next turn. If she succeeds, she casts the spell normally and it is not expended from her prepared spell slot, allowing her to cast that spell again at a later point." usage="A number of times/day equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› her druid level"}

::ab[Nature]{sub icon=magic-def-lower note="A progenitor who chooses nature as her primal bond can create plants." order=nru fullround="At the start of her next turn, so long as she is touching a solid surface underfoot, a 5-foot radius of mobile undergrowth springs to life around the progenitor for a number of rounds equal to 3 plus the progenitor's Wisdom bonus. Upon activating this ability, the progenitor chooses whether the undergrowth provides ‹rule/cover› or ‹rule/difficult terrain›. If the progenitor chooses cover, her undergrowth provides a cover bonus to AC to the progenitor and allies within the radius. If she chooses difficult terrain, enemies who enter the radius of undergrowth treat it as difficult terrain. A progenitor's undergrowth is treated as forest terrain if it provides cover and plains terrain if it provides difficult terrain. This radius increases by 5 feet at 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter." usage="A number of times/day equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› her druid level"}

::ab[Fey Shape (Su)]{icon=down-boost l=4 note="A progenitor never gains the ability to use *wild shape* to transform into an elemental." imp6="A progenitor can use *wild shape* to change into a Small or Medium fey. When doing so, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/fey form I›." imp8="A progenitor can use *wild shape* to change into a Tiny or Large fey. When she does so, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/fey form II›." imp10="A progenitor can use *wild shape* to change into a Diminutive or Huge fey. When she does so, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/fey form III›." imp14="When a progenitor uses *wild shape* to assume a fey form, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/fey form IV›." alter="Wild shape"}

reincarnated_druid.name: Reincarnated Druid
description: ## Reincarnated Druid
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/39›

Spun off into the endless circle of life, an incarnate druid is an embodiment of nature's eternal renewal. She lives many lives and wanders the world devoid of attachments, a stranger to all yet one with all life.

::ab[Mysterious Stranger (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A reincarnated druids adds ‹misc/half› her druid level to the DC of Sense Motive, Diplomacy, and Knowledge checks to learn about her." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Resist Death's Touch (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A reincarnated druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against death effects, energy drain, and necromancy effects, and on stabilization checks when dying." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Many Lives (Ex)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="If a reincarnated druid is killed, she may automatically ‹spell/reincarnate› (as the spell) 1 day later. The reincarnated druid appears in a safe location within 1 mile of her previous body. At will for the next 7 days, she can sense the presence of her remains as if using ‹spell/locate object› as a spell-like ability. If she is killed during these 7 days, she remains dead and does not reincarnate." special="The many lives ability does not function if the reincarnated druid is slain by a death effect. A reincarnated druid cannot be ‹spell/raise«d from the» dead› or ‹spell/resurrect«ed<ion›, though she can be reincarnated."}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down ability="A reincarnated druid gains *wild shape,* but it functions at her druid level - 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Cheat Death (Ex)]{l=9 icon=roll ability="Once per day, a reincarnated druid may reroll a save against a death effect, energy drain, or necromancy effect before the result of the roll is revealed, or reroll a failed stabilization check while dying. She must take the result of the second roll, even if it is worse than the original roll." replace="Venom immunity"}
::ab[Tongue of the Sun and Moon (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power ability="A reincarnated druid can speak with any living creature." replace="Timeless body"}

restorer.name: Restorer
description: ## Restorer
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/22›
Restorers carefully tend to the health of both the environment and creatures in it. Restorers in the most remote places often choose druidic herbalism as their nature bond option.
::ab[Healing Touch (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A restorer gains a +2 bonus on Heal checks. When a restorer uses Heal to provide first aid, the bonus increases to +3." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Natural Medic]{icon=magic ability="A restorer can channel stored spell energy into healing spells that she did not prepare ahead of time. The restorer can &quot&lose&quot& any prepared spell in order to cast any ‹main/cure spell<s› she can cast of the same spell level or lower (a *cure* spell is any spell with &quot&cure&quot& in its name)." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Enemy of Blight (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A restorer can ignore the effects of an overgrown area that has been magically manipulated to impede motion." usage="She can ignore these effects for a number of rounds per day equal to double her Wisdom bonus. These rounds don't have to be used consecutively." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Guide across Lifetimes (Sp)]{l=13 icon=magic fullround="Once per day, a restorer can cast ‹spell/reincarnate› without a material component." replace="A thousand faces"}

river_druid.name: River Druid
description: ## River Druid
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/48;People of the River/14›

These druids are the guardians of rivers and the creatures that depend on them. Just as rivers pour from mountains to seas, river druids adapt to many environments and cultures. They can be found wherever water flows, even among underground rivers or sewer systems.

::ab[Ferrier (Ex)]{icon=skill-boost note="A river druid adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA) to her list of class skills and gains a +2 bonus on Diplomacy and Knowledge (nature) skill checks." alter="Class skills" replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Read the Currents (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="A river druid gains a bonus equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level on initiative checks and Acrobatics, Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, Survival, and Swim checks when she is in, on, or adjacent to flowing water." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Countercurrent (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A river druid takes no penalty to speed or on Acrobatics or Stealth checks in shallow water up to 2 feet deep (1 foot deep for Small characters) such as shallow bogs, shorelines, streams, and partially flooded areas. A river druid (and any vehicle she pilots) isn't moved by flowing water unless she allows it. Water that has been magically manipulated to impede or boost motion still affects her." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Deep Breath (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A river druid can ‹misc/hold her breath› for a number of minutes equal to her Constitution score (after this, she must begin attempting Constitution checks to avoid suffocation as normal)." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down ability="A river druid gains *wild shape,* except her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}
::ab[Tongue of the Sun and Moon (Ex)]{l=15 icon=power passive="A river druid can speak with any living creature." replace="Timeless body"}

road_keeper.name: Road Keeper
description: ## Road Keeper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/48›
Guardians of the lonely paths between cities, road keepers protect the wild from travelers, and travelers from the wild.

::ab[One with the Road (Su)]{icon=boost note="A road keeper casts the following spells as though her caster level were 1 higher." s1=Longstrider s2="Scent trail" s3="Nature's exile" s4="Freedom of movement" s5="Tree stride" s6="Find the path" s7="Wind walk" s8="Word of recall" s9="World wave" imp9="The bonus to her caster level for these spells increases to +2." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Road Bond (Ex)]{icon=info choice="If the road keeper chooses a cleric domain as her nature bond, she must choose from the ‹domain/Community›, ‹domain/Liberation›, ‹domain/Protection›, ‹domain/Travel›, or ‹domain/Weather› domains. The road keeper can also choose any subdomain associated with these domains." alter="Nature bond" replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Take the High Road (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost ability="A road keeper travels over roads and paths at an accelerated rate. She and her allies can ‹rule/hustle› for 2 hours without taking nonlethal damage, instead of 1, provided they travel along a road or path. Additionally, as long as they travel on a road or path, the road keeper and her allies count as traveling along a highway regardless of the quality of the road or path on which they travel. Allies must remain within 30 feet of the road keeper to benefit from this ability." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Trodden Path (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-def passive="A road keeper gains a +4 bonus on Survival checks to follow or identify tracks. She also gains this bonus on saving throws against severe weather effects." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Road Less Traveled (Su)]{l=4 icon=lower flavor="A road keeper can discourage creatures from entering or exiting the wild." standard="Once per day, she can target a creature within 30 feet and curse it with clumsiness and disorientation. The road keeper chooses whether to punish her target for entering the wild from a road or building, or vice versa. If her target is entering the wild, it treats every square of wilderness not on a road or in a building as ‹rule/difficult terrain›. If her target is exiting the wild, it treats every square on a road or inside a building as difficult terrain. In either case, the ground feels uneven and the target suffers extreme vertigo during movement. The victim of this curse can attempt a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + ‹misc/half› the road keeper's level + her Wisdom modifier) at the beginning of its turn each round to end the effect. The effect lasts until the target successfully saves or for a number of minutes equal to the road keeper's level." special="A creature's movement isn't affected if it is burrowing, climbing, earth gliding, flying, or swimming. This is a mind-affecting curse effect." imp6="At 6th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the road keeper can target an additional creature per day with this ability (to a maximum number of nine targets per day at 20th level). She can target more than one creature with a single use of this ability or the same target more than once in the same day (though its effects do not stack), but her total number of targets per day cannot exceed this daily maximum. If she targets more than one creature at one time, each target must be within 30 feet of the road keeper." replace="Wild shape"}

rot_warden.name: Rot Warden
description: ## Rot Warden
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/49›

Harbingers of decay, rot wardens live in damp lands where insects and rot break down the dead to feed the living. They see in the process of rot the beauty of nature's capacity to recycle and repurpose material into something wondrous and new.

::ab[Invoke Decay (Su)]{icon=magic ability="A rot warden can channel stored spell energy into specific spells he hasn't prepared ahead of time. He can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell in order to cast the following spells at the indicated spell levels." s1="Decompose corpse" s2="Warp wood" s3="Fungal infestation" s4="Explosion of rot" s5="Insect plague" s6="Swarm skin" s7="Creeping doom" s8="Horrid wilting" s9=Massacre alter="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A rot warden who chooses a cleric domain as his nature bond must choose from the ‹domain/Destruction›, ‹druiddomain/Erosion›, ‹domain/Repose›, or ‹druiddomain/Vermin› domains." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Vermin Empathy (Su)]{icon=power ability="A rot warden can improve the attitude of vermin as a normal druid can with animals. Vermin have a starting attitude of unfriendly. The rot warden can also improve the attitude of vermin swarms, but he takes a -4 penalty on the check unless the swarm consists of the same kind of vermin as his vermin companion or his current *wild shape.*" replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Swarmcaller (Sp)]{l=3 icon=magic standard="A rot warden can summon a swarm of vermin once per day. This functions like ‹spell/summon swarm›, except the swarm the rot warden summons must be a ‹monster/cockroach<_swarm›, ‹monster/locust<_swarm›, or ‹monster/spider swarm›. If he uses this ability during the surprise round of combat, nearby animals and vermin become hushed until the first round of combat ends; during this surprise round and first round of combat, ‹rule/flat-footed› creatures also gain the ‹misc/shaken› condition (this doesn't stack with other fear effects)." imp9="He adds ‹monster/army ant<_swarm›, ‹monster/centipede<_swarm›, and ‹monster/wasp swarm«s› to the list of swarms he can call with this ability." replace="Trackless step~venom immunity"}

::ab[Enduring Druid (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A rot warden gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against attacks and special abilities of vermin and swarms with the vermin type. This bonus also applies on saving throws against effects that would age or decay the rot warden or his equipment." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape]{l=6 icon=down-boost ability="A rot warden gains *wild shape,* except his effective druid level is his druid level - 2. He cannot take the form of an elemental." imp8="A rot warden can assume the form of a Small or Medium vermin as if using ‹spell/vermin shape I›." imp10="The rot warden can assume the shape of a Tiny or Large vermin as if using ‹spell/vermin shape II›." imp12="He can take the form of a Huge vermin as if using vermin shape III.@FN1" replace="Wild shape"}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[No such spell was ever published. A similar spell could be made based on ‹spell/beast shape III› and *vermin shape II* - consult your GM.]{from=1}

saurian_shaman.name: Saurian Shaman
description: ## Saurian Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/39›

A shaman with this focus calls upon the primeval dinosaur, the archaic terror that lingers as a hungering, atavistic stranger at the fringes of the ecosystem, a destroyer and despoiler whose coming other animals dread.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A saurian shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a dinosaur. If choosing a domain, a saurian shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Destruction›, ‹domain/Strength›, and ‹domain/War› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A saurian shaman can use *wild empathy* with dinosaurs and reptiles as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A saurian shaman may adopt an aspect of the saurian while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (+10 enhancement bonus to land speed)
- **Scales** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d6], 2 claws [1d4] for a Medium druid, rake, +2 CMB to grapple)
While using *totem transformation,* a saurian shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (reptiles and dinosaurs only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A saurian shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon reptiles and dinosaurs, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to such summons to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost ability="A saurian shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a reptile or a dinosaur, she instead uses her druid level +2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A saurian shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Improved Overrun›, ‹feat/Nimble Moves›, ‹feat/Power Attack›, ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Intimidate), or ‹feat/Vital Strike›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

season_keeper.name: Season Keeper
description: ## Season Keeper
‹SOURCE People of the Stars/13›

Imbued with the primeval potency of their planet's seasons, those druids known as season keepers guide Triaxian communities through the stark transition from summer to winter (and vice versa). The nature spirits that season keepers allow to possess their animal companions inspire both awe and dread in onlookers, but all value the gifts of the seasons that the season keepers bestow on their communities.
This archetype is available to ‹race/Triaxian› druids, as well as druids of other races with the GM's permission.
::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=info choice="A season keeper must select an animal companion for her nature bond."}
::ab[Seasonal Spirits (Su)]{head icon=magic l=1 ability="Each day when she prepares spells, a season keeper can imbue her animal companion with the spirit of summer or the spirit of winter."}

::ab[Spirit of Summer]{next icon=aura-boost passive="The season keeper's animal companion projects a 15-foot-radius aura of cooling and vigor. Allies in the aura can exist comfortably in hot conditions as if they each had the *seasoned* racial ability of a Summerborn Triaxian." imp3="The aura increases the power of the season keeper's healing magic - whenever the season keeper targets an ally with a spell that has the healing descriptor, the target also gains ‹umr/fast healing› 2 for a number of rounds equal to the spell's level." imp9="Allies within the aura leave no trails in natural grassy or forest environments, thus can't be tracked in such terrain." imp15="The duration of the fast healing effect increases to 2 rounds per spell level and affected allies can ignore ‹rule/difficult terrain› caused by natural undergrowth such as nonmagical thorns, briars, overgrowth, and similar terrain."}

::ab[Spirit of Winter]{next icon=aura-def passive="The season keeper's animal companion projects a 15-foot-radius aura of warmth and calm. This aura allows allies to exist comfortably in cold conditions as if they each had the *seasoned* racial ability of a Winterborn Triaxian." imp3="Allies gain ‹umr/resistance› 10 to cold as long as they remain within the aura." imp9="Affected allies leave no trails in natural snow or ice, thus can't be tracked in such terrain." imp15="Affected allies gain resistance 20 to cold and can ignore difficult terrain caused by ice or snow." alter="Nature bond" replace="Trackless step~venom immunity~timeless body"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down ability="A season keeper gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her druid level - 2. If she has imbued her animal companion with the *spirit of winter,* she uses her full druid level when taking on the form of an ice elemental. If she has imbued her companion with the *spirit of summer,* she uses her full druid level when taking on the form of a plant." special="A season keeper cannot use *wild shape* to assume the form of an elemental except an ice elemental, and can assume that shape only if her animal companion has the *spirit of winter.* Similarly, she can assume a plant form only if her animal companion has the *spirit of summer.*" alter="Wild shape"}

season_sage.name: Season Sage
description: ## Season Sage
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/11›
Season sages wander as the seasons change, turning the leaves and transforming nature as they pass and taking pleasure in the transition between seasons.
::prereq{r=Gathlain}

::ab[Season's Touch (Su)]{head l=4 icon=touch ability="A season sage can infuse living things with the essence of a season with a touch, causing them to manifest the changes associated with that season - for example, a touch of winter can make some animals grow a thick winter coat, while a touch of summer can make them shed. A touch of autumn would make a deciduous tree's leaves change colors, a touch of winter would make its leaves fall, and a touch of spring would make it put forth new leaves." order=an note="In addition, a season sage can use the following abilities (in any combination) a total number of times per day equal to 1 + his Wisdom modifier." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Vernal Growth]{next icon=boost ability="A touched living creature gains the effects of ‹spell/enlarge person› (even if it is not a humanoid) for 1 minute per druid level. A touched non-creature plant likewise doubles in all dimensions for the same duration."}
::ab[Summer Might]{next icon=boost ability="A touched creature gains a +4 enhancement bonus to Constitution for 1 minute per druid level."}
::ab[Autumn Rot]{next icon=touch ability="A touched creature or object made of plant matter takes 1d6 points of acid damage per 2 druid levels."}
::ab[Winter Frost]{next icon=touch ability="A touched creature or object takes 1d6 points of cold damage per 2 druid levels."}

::ab[Season's Veil (Su)]{head icon=aura l=6 standard="A season sage can radiate an aura of seasonal power. This affects a 20-foot-radius ‹misc/burst› centered on the season sage and lasts in that area for 10 minutes, regardless of where the season sage moves afterward." useInc="2~druid~6" imp10="The radius increases to 40 feet."}

::ab[Vernal Bloom]{next icon=magic ability="The area surges with life. Plants form vibrant, ‹rule/heavy undergrowth»<forest_terrain› while in the aura. If the area can support plants, a circle of minor plants lingers afterward." imp8="The season sage can affect the area with ‹spell/plant growth› as a @HLswift_action at will, which can linger after the aura passes."}

::ab[Summer Heat]{next icon=magic ability="The area of effect is treated as if it were under the effects of ‹rule/extreme heat»<heat_dangers›." imp8="As a @HLswift_action the season sage can have the area deal 1d6 points of fire damage each round to other creatures in the area."}

::ab[Autumn Squall]{next icon=magic ability="The area is filled with fog, rain, and blown leaves. All creatures in the area gain ‹rule/concealment› (abilities to specifically see through fog or rain don't apply)." imp8="The season sage can affect the area with ‹spell/diminish plants› as a @HLswift_action at will, which also lingers after the aura passes."}

::ab[Winter Chill]{next icon=magic ability="The area of effect is treated as if it were under the effects of ‹rule/extreme cold»<cold_dangers›." imp8="As a @HLswift_action the season sage can have the area deal 1d6 points of cold damage each round to other creatures in the area."}
::ab[Season Mastery (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic ability="A season sage can cast ‹spell/control weather› as a spell-like ability once per day; his choice of effects is not limited by the season."}

serpent_shaman.name: Serpent Shaman
description: ## Serpent Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/103›

A shaman with this totem calls upon the cunning serpent, the stealthy deceiver who draws the weak minded in and strikes while they are unaware. Some hate its treacherous nature, while others praise its thoughtful pragmatism.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A serpent shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a snake. If choosing a domain, the serpent shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Charm›, ‹domain/Trickery›, and ‹domain/Water› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A serpent shaman can use *wild empathy* with reptiles as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A serpent shaman may adopt an aspect of the snake while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (climb speed 20 feet, swim speed 20 feet)
- **Scales** (+2 natural armor bonus to AC)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d4] and poison for a Medium druid, +2 CMB to grapple)
::aff[Serpent Shaman Venom]{iconP poison freqR=6 effCon=1 cure1 dcF dcLev=druid dcMod=Con dcIt=her}
While using *totem transformation,* the serpent shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (reptiles only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A serpent shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon snakes, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any snake to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A serpent shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a snake, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A serpent shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Combat Expertise›, ‹feat/Improved Feint›, ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Bluff), ‹feat/Stealthy›, or ‹feat/Strike Back›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

shark_shaman.name: Shark Shaman
description: ## Shark Shaman
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/40›
Some druids emulate the deadly shark, a remorseless hunter that marine dwellers dread. Like a true shark, a shark shaman leaves blood and fear in her wake.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A shark shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/shark›. If choosing a domain, a shark shaman may choose the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Death›, ‹domain/War›, or ‹domain/Water› domain." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A shark shaman can use *wild empathy* with fish as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A shark shaman may adopt an aspect of the shark while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (can breathe water, swim speed 30 feet)
- **Senses** (‹umr/scent› 30 feet, scent 90 feet in water)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite 1d6 for a Medium shaman)
- **Shark skin** (+2 natural armor, creatures grappling the shaman take 1 point of slashing damage per round of grapple)
While using *totem transformation,* the shark shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (fish only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost standard="A shark shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally I› to summon a ‹monster/stingray› and ‹spell/summon nature's ally II› to summon a ‹monster/manta ray›. Summoned rays and sharks gain a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any such summon to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A shark shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a shark, she instead uses her druid level +2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A shark shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Bleeding Critical›, ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Lightning Reflexes›, ‹feat/Self-Sufficient›, or ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Swim)." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

skinshaper.name: Skinshaper
description: ## Skinshaper
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/26›

Using the same primal energy with which other druids assume animal forms, skinshapers can imitate some of the most dangerous beasts of all: humans and other humanoids. Rather than merely copying humanoids' shapes and abilities, skinshapers unlock brutal energies that push their borrowed bodies far beyond the capabilities of normal humanoid creatures. Their connection to humanoids bring skinshapers into settlements more often than typical druids, and their penchant for disguise allows them to excel at intrigue.
::cskill{gain=dis lose=ride}

::ab[Skinshaping (Su)]{l=4 head icon=magic ability="A skinshaper gains the ability to turn herself into any Small or Medium humanoid and back again once per day. This ability functions as per ‹spell/alter self›, except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per druid level, or until the skinshaper changes back. Changing form is a @HLstandard_action and doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity. The skinshaper must be familiar with the form she has chosen." special="She also gains other benefits while *skinshaping,* as described below." useInc="2~druid~4" imp20="She can use *skinshaping* at will." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Unarmed Strike]{next icon=melee l4="The skinshaper learns to intuitively use her body as a weapon. While *skinshaping,* she gains the benefit of the ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike› feat, and her unarmed strike deals damage as if she were a ‹class/monk› with a monk level equal to her druid level - 3."}

::ab[Enhancement Bonus]{next icon=boost l6="A skinshaper gains the ability to channel the versatile nature of humanoids to enhance her body and mind. Whenever she uses *skinshaping* to assume a humanoid shape other than her own, she gains a +2 enhancement bonus to any one ability score. The bonus persists as long as she remains in that form." imp10="She gains an additional +2 enhancement bonus to one ability score, or can stack them as a +4 bonus to one score." imp14="She gains a third +2 enhancement bonus to one ability score. She can distribute these bonuses as she wishes in increments of +2, but the enhancement bonus on any single ability score cannot exceed +4." imp18="She gains a fourth +2 enhancement bonus to one ability score, with the same restrictions as above."}

::ab[Advanced Form]{next icon=power l8="A skinshaper can imitate humanoid creatures even more accurately. She gains any of the following abilities that the form she assumes has: darkvision 90 feet, low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›, climb 60 feet, fly 60 feet (good maneuverability), swim 60 feet, ‹umr/ferocity›, ‹race/half_>orc« ferocity›, and ‹umr/hold breath›. If the form she assumes has the ‹subtype/aquatic› subtype, she gains the aquatic subtype and the ‹umr/amphibious› special quality."}

::ab[Racial Memory]{next icon=power l12="A skinshaper can access the racial memory of the humanoid she is imitating. If the form she assumes has any of the following racial abilities, she gains those abilities: defensive training, hatred, ‹ability/poison use›, ‹ability/sneak attack› +2d6, stability, and weapon familiarity. Additionally, she can speak any racial languages of the chosen form."}

::ab[Skill Bonuses]{next icon=boost l16="A skinshaper gains the racial skill bonuses of the imitated humanoid, to a maximum of a +4 bonus on a given skill. The skinshaper cannot benefit from any variable racial skill bonuses associated with the chosen form (such as gnomes' racial bonus on any one Craft or Profession skill)."}
::ab[Flashmorph (Su)]{l=13 icon=boost note="A skinshaper learns to change her shape extremely quickly. She can use skinshaping as a @HLswift_action." replace="A thousand faces"}

sky_druid.name: Sky Druid
description: ## Sky Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/158›

Some druids develop ties not to a particular landscape, but instead to the endless blue expanse of the skies. Such are the sky druids, who are more at home soaring through air than standing on the ground. A sky druid has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Sylph}
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A sky druid loses medium armor proficiency."}

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=info choice="A sky druid who chooses an animal companion must select one with a fly speed. If choosing a domain, the sky druid must choose from the ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Liberation›, and ‹domain/Weather› domains, or subdomains appropriate to those domains." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Sky's Embrace (Su)]{l=2 icon=def-power passive="A sky druid no longer takes falling damage, as though she were constantly under the effect of ‹spell/feather fall›." ability="Additionally, she may take ranks in the Fly skill regardless of whether she has a natural fly speed, and may use her Fly skill in place of Acrobatics when making jump checks." order=pa replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Resist Storm (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A sky druid gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against spells with the air or electricity descriptors and against effects that control or modify the weather (such as ‹spell/sleet storm›)." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Skymaster (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A sky druid can use the ‹spell/fly› spell (self only) for a number of minutes per day equal to her druid level. These minutes do not need to be consecutive." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=power-boost ability="A sky druid gains the ability to use *wild shape.* When a sky druid takes the form of a creature with a fly speed, this ability functions at her class level + 1. For all other forms, her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her actual sky druid level. This ability otherwise functions as *wild shape.*" replace="Wild shape"}
::ab[Soaring Form (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A sky druid is no longer affected by ‹rule/altitude sickness› or natural or magical wind." replace="Venom immunity"}
::ab[Into the Wild Blue (Su)]{l=13 icon=power ability="A sky druid gains a fly speed equal to twice her base land speed (good maneuverability)." replace="A thousand faces"}

storm_druid.name: Storm Druid
description: ## Storm Druid
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/40›

While most druids focus their attention upon the rich earth and the bounty of nature that springs forth from it, the storm druid's eyes have ever been cast to the skies and the endless expanse of blue, channeling the most raw and untamed aspects of nature.

::ab[Spontaneous Domain Casting]{icon=magic ability="A storm druid can channel stored spell energy into domain spells that she has not prepared ahead of time. She can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell in order to cast any domain spell of the same level or lower." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=down choice="A storm druid may not choose an animal companion. A storm druid must choose the ‹domain/Air› or ‹domain/Weather› domain, or the Cloud, Storm, or Wind subdomain."}
::ab[Windwalker (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="The penalties from natural or magical wind effects are treated as one step less severe for a storm druid." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Stormvoice (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A storm druid's voice can magically carry over howling winds and peals of thunder. Whenever a Perception check is needed to hear the druid's voice, the DC is reduced by an amount equal to the druid's level." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Eyes of the Storm (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A storm druid can see through 10 feet of magical fog, mist, gas, wind, rain, or similar inclement weather conditions, ignoring any concealment it might grant." incrementPlain="Her sight~4~4~15~5" incrementDesc="can penetrate~increases to" incrementEnd=" feet." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Windlord]{l=9 icon=power choice="A storm druid can select another domain or subdomain from those available to her through her nature bond." replace="Venom immunity"}

::ab[Storm Lord (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="A storm druid is unaffected by natural and magical wind effects. She also becomes immune to deafness and gains +2 bonus on saving throws against sonic effects." replace="A thousand faces"}

sunrider.name: Sunrider
description: ## Sunrider
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/13›

Sunriders help their allies, be they fellow tribe members or fellow adventurers, to survive in harsh desert terrain, while simultaneously making it even harsher for their foes. They seem to share a single mind with their loyal steeds, and companions who ride with them become an eerily well-coordinated cavalry unit that traverses the treacherous desert sands with ease to wreak havoc upon invaders. Sunriders almost never leave their tribal homelands; those found outside Al-Zabrit are usually either exiles or emissaries sent to deal with issues that can't be addressed from their holds.
::cskill{gain=kl lose=prof}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiencies]{icon=power-down ability="A sunrider gains proficiency with the ‹eq-weapon/shortbow›, but not with the scythe, sickle, or quarterstaff." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=down choice="A sunrider must use this ability to bond with a ‹companion/horse› or a ‹companion/pony› as an animal companion; she cannot choose a different animal or choose a domain instead of an animal companion." alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Born to the Saddle (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost passive="While wearing light or no armor, a sunrider does not need to attempt Ride checks for any task listed in the Ride skill with a DC of 15 or lower. She does not take an armor check penalty on Ride checks while riding her mount." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Mounted Advantage (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost-aura passive="Any allied mount within 30 feet of a sunrider can ignore the effects of rocky or sandy ‹rule/difficult terrain›." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Desert Born (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-aura flavor="A sunrider is at home in the desert." passive="She gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and on Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, and Survival checks while she is in desert terrain. Mounted allies traveling with her likewise gain a +1 bonus on initiative checks and Perception and Survival checks while in desert terrain, as long as they are within 30 feet of the sunrider." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Concerted Effort (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power-aura-def-boost flavor="A sunrider can extend the bond she shares with her horse to other nearby mounted companions, forming them into a fighting force that seems to share a single mind." passive="Allied mounts within 60 feet of the sunrider gain ‹ability/evasion› as per the ‹sidekick/animal companion› special ability." imp6="The range expands to 90 feet and allied mounts gain a +10-foot enhancement bonus to their speed." imp8="The range expands to 120 feet and allied mounts gain *devotion* as per the animal companion special ability." imp10="Allied mounts gain *multiattack* as per the animal companion special ability." imp12="Allied mounts gain a +2 bonus on saving throws." imp14="Allied mounts gain a +20-foot enhancement bonus to their speed." imp16="Allied mounts gain ‹ability/improved evasion› as per the animal companion special ability." imp18="Allied mounts gain a dodge bonus to their Armor Class and a +2 bonus on attack rolls." imp20="Allied mounts gain DR 5/- and energy resistance 10 to acid, cold, electricity, fire, and sonic damage." special="These abilities function only while allied mounts are within range of the sunrider (her own mount always counts as an ally)." replace="Wild shape"}

supernaturalist.name: Supernaturalist
description: ## Supernaturalist
‹SOURCE Occult Origins/21›
Supernaturalists wholly embrace paranormal phenomena as extensions of nature.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=down note="A supernaturalist is not proficient with any type of armor." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}

::ab[Eldritch Botanist (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A supernaturalist can affect plants with mind-affecting effects she creates and with spells that normally affect animals. Any such spell that allows a Will save can instead be negated by the target plant with a successful Fortitude save at the same DC."}

::ab[Paranormal Scholar (Ex)]{icon=power-skill-learn ability="A supernaturalist gains ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as a bonus feat and adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA), ‹skill/Knowledge (arcana)› (INT), and ‹skill/Sense Motive› (WIS) as class skills." special="At 3rd level and every 3 levels thereafter, she can select a spell from the ‹main/spells_>psychic« class's spell list› and add it to her druid spell list, and is able to prepare and cast it as a druid spell." replace="Nature sense~trackless step~resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Animal Spirit (Su)]{l=4 head icon=power ability="A supernaturalist can perform a seance to call an animal spirit of any animal with which she is familiar to serve as her companion and guide. The supernaturalist gains *spirit, spirit bonus, spirit surge,* and *taboo* abilities as a ‹class/medium› 3 levels lower than her druid level. The supernaturalist's allies cannot participate in this seance." special="The spirit abilities of the animal spirit are given below." replace="Wild shape"}
::ab[Spirit Bonus]{next icon=boost note="Your spirit bonus applies on attack and damage rolls with natural weapons and on skill checks relating to animals and plants."}
::ab[Seance Boon]{next icon=def passive="Your natural armor bonus to AC increases by 1."}
::ab[Favored Location]{next icon=info note="Native terrain of the animal type being invoked."}

::ab[Influence Penalty]{next icon=down passive="You become wild and savage, taking a penalty equal to your spirit bonus on caster level checks, Charisma- and Intelligence-based ability checks, and skill checks."}
:::ab{next action=Choice icon=down title=Taboos}
Choose one:
- You must not speak (except with animals and plants)
- You must not wield manufactured weapons
- You must not eat anything you did not kill or harvest yourself
:::ab{next title="Totem Transformation (Lesser, Su)" icon=boost action=Ability}
You gain any of the following abilities possessed by the animal you select as your animal spirit:
- ‹umr/natural_weapons>«Bite› (1d6 for a Medium druid)
- 2 claws (1d4 for a Medium druid)
- Darkvision
- Improved land movement speed (up to a +20-foot enhancement bonus to the druid's base land movement speed)
- Low-light vision
- ‹umr/Scent›
- Swim (up to 30 feet).
::ab[Pack Leader (Intermediate, Su)]{next icon=boost passive="When you summon animals that match your animal spirit, they gain a +2 bonus on attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws."}

::ab[Wildstrike (Greater, Su)]{next icon=power swift="You can allow your animal spirit to gain 1 point of influence over you to allow an animal or plant (including your companion) within 30 feet that can see or hear you to immediately take an additional standard action."}

::ab[Wild Heart (Supreme, Ex)]{icon=def-magic order=pa passive="You gain a +4 bonus on saves against enchantment and mind-affecting effects, and immunity to effects that affect only humanoids." ability="Once per day, you can cast any ‹spell/summon nature's ally› spell you can cast spontaneously as a @HLstandard_action without expending a spell slot."}
::h3[Optional Abilities]{jl}
A supernaturalist can choose *plant bond* and *green empathy* in place of *nature bond* and *wild empathy.* This choice must be made when the druid takes this archetype.
::ab[Plant Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power head choice="A supernaturalist forms a mystic bond with plant life. This bond can take one of two forms." replace="Nature bond"}

::ab[Domain]{sub icon=learn-magic ability="This grants the supernaturalist one of the following domains: ‹domain/Plant›, ‹domain/Growth›, ‹druiddomain/Jungle›, ‹druiddomain/Swamp›. When determining the powers and bonus spells granted by this domain, the supernaturalist's effective cleric level is equal to her druid level. A supernaturalist who selects this option also receives additional domain spell slots, just like a cleric. She must prepare the spell from her domain in this slot, and this spell cannot be used to cast a spell spontaneously."}

::ab[Plant companion]{sub icon=power ability="A supernaturalist can form a close bond with a plant companion. A supernaturalist may begin play with any of the plants listed under **Plant Companions** in the ‹arc-druid/treesinger› archetype. This plant is a loyal companion that accompanies the supernaturalist on her adventures. Except for the companion being a creature of the plant type, drawn from the list of plant companions, this ability otherwise works like the standard druid's animal companion ability."}
:::ab{title="Green Empathy (Ex)" l=1 icon=power action=Ability replace="Wild empathy"}}

A supernaturalist can improve the attitude of a plant creature. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check made to improve the attitude of a person. The supernaturalist rolls 1d20 and adds her druid level and her Charisma modifier to determine the *green empathy* check result. The typical wild plant creature has a starting attitude of indifferent.

To use *green empathy,* the supernaturalist and the plant creature must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing a plant creature in this way takes 1 minute but, as with influencing people, it might take more or less time.
A supernaturalist can also use this ability to influence an animal, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check.

survivor.name: Survivor
description: ## Survivor
‹SOURCE Champions of Balance/24›
The survivor embodies the neutral aspect of the natural world and eschews druidic magic in favor of more pragmatic and worldly survival methods.
::cskill{gain=stl}
::ab[Weapon Proficiencies]{icon=power ability="In addition to the normal druid weapon proficiencies, a survivor is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/shortbow› and ‹eq-weapon/longbow›."}

::ab[Diminished Spellcasting]{icon=down note="A survivor's number of spells per day for each spell level is one less than normal (for example, a 4th-level survivor can cast three cantrips, two 2nd-level spells, and one 1st-level spell per day). If this reduces the number of spells for a level to 0, she gains only the bonus spells for that level she would be entitled to based on her Wisdom score." alter=Spellcasting}
::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=down choice="A survivor must select an animal companion as her *nature bond.*" alter="Nature bond"}

::ab[Element of Surprise (Ex)]{l=4 icon=lower ability="If a survivor makes a successful attack during a surprise round against a target that is unaware of her, her victim takes a penalty on its initiative check during the following round equal to half the damage dealt by the survivor's attack (minimum 0, maximum equal to the survivor's class level). This cannot reduce an initiative result below 1." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Trap]{l=4 icon=power ability="A survivor learns how to create a snare trap and one other ‹ability/ranger trap<s› of her choice. At 6th level and every 2 levels thereafter, she learns another trap. The survivor can use these traps a total number of times per day equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level + her Wisdom modifier. Once a trap is learned, it can't be unlearned and replaced with a different type of trap. The survivor cannot select an individual trap more than once." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Launch Trap]{l=9 icon=ranged ability="A survivor can affix a magical ranger trap to an arrow or thrown weapon, allowing her to set the trap remotely or use it as a direct attack. Attaching the trap to the projectile is part of the @HLfull-round_action of creating a new trap. The trapped projectile is fired or thrown in the normal manner. If the projectile is fired at a square, the trap is treated as if the survivor had set the trap in that square, except the DC is 5 lower than normal. If the projectile is fired at a creature, the target takes damage from the ranged weapon and is treated as if it had triggered the trap (saving throw applies, if any)." special="The attack has a maximum range of 60 feet, and range increments apply to the attack roll. The duration of the trapped projectile starts from when it is created, not from when it is used." replace="Venom immunity"}

swamp_druid.name: Swamp Druid
description: ## Swamp Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/100›

Some druids eschew pleasant glades and groves and instead seek out dank marshes, misty bogs and heaths, and trackless swamps as the place they call home and watch over with care, finding beauty and life in abundance in places few others would willingly enter.

::ab[Marshwight (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost-power passive="A swamp druid gains a bonus on Initiative checks and Knowledge (geography), Perception, Stealth, Swim, and Survival checks equal to ‹misc/half› her druid level in swamp terrain, and she cannot be tracked in such an environment." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Swamp Strider (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A swamp druid suffers no penalty to speed or on Acrobatics or Stealth checks in bogs and undergrowth." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Pond Scum (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="A swamp druid gains a +4 bonus on saves against disease and the exceptional, supernatural, and spell-like abilities of monstrous humanoids. A swamp druid also gains DR/- equal to half her druid level against attacks by swarms. If this damage resistance prevents damage, the druid is unaffected by distraction or other special attacks of the swarm." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down ability="A swamp druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for this ability is equal to her druid level - 2." alter="Wild shape"}
::ab[Slippery (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def-boost passive="A swamp druid gains continuous ‹spell/freedom of movement›." replace="A thousand faces"}

swarm_monger.name: Swarm Monger
description: ## Swarm Monger
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/21›

Swarm mongers are unparalleled survivors, thriving on the filthy fringes of society. Whereas other druids commune with nature or even the spirit of a city, swarm mongers find beauty and strength in decay, and they draw their power from fungus, disease, and their own singular will to survive.
::prereq{r=Ratfolk}

::ab[Fecund Familiar (Ex)]{icon=power order=cpsu choice="A swarm monger bonds with an urban ‹sidekick/familiar›, treating her druid level as her ‹class/wizard› level for the purposes of determining her familiar's abilities. The swarm monger must select her familiar from the ‹ability/following options»<familiars›: cat, house centipede, rat, raven, or scarlet spider." passive="The fecund familiar gains the benefits of its master's *child of pollution, shadowy opportunist,* and *venom immunity* class abilities." standard="A swarm monger can cause her familiar to burst forth into a full swarm of identical creatures, filling four contiguous 5-foot squares and gaining ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to ‹misc/half› its master's maximum hit points. While in swarm form, the familiar loses the *improved evasion, share spells, deliver touch attack,* and *scry on familiar* special abilities. It uses its normal AC, saving throws, and skill bonuses, and it gains the ‹subtype/swarm› subtype and the ability to make swarm attacks (dealing 1d6 points of damage at 1st level and using the swarm monger's druid level as the swarm's Hit Dice to determine damage increases as per the swarm subtype)." imp5="Any creature damaged by a fecund familiar's swarm attack must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the swarm monger's druid level + the swarm monger's Wisdom modifier) or be ‹misc/sickened› for 1d6 rounds." imp12="Creatures that fail their Fortitude saving throws instead become ‹misc/nauseated›." usage="A swarm monger can transform her familiar into a swarm a number of times each day equal to her Wisdom modifier (minimum 1), and the transformation lasts for 1 minute per druid level, or until the swarm monger reverts her familiar back to a singular form as a @HLstandard_action." special="The swarm monger is immune to her own familiar's swarm attack. A fecund familiar cannot be reduced in size to Diminutive or smaller when in swarm form." replace="Nature bond"}
::ab[Low Friends (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A swarm monger gains ‹feat/Vermin Heart› as a bonus feat." replace="Woodland stride"}
::ab[Shadowy Opportunist (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A swarm monger gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (local) and Stealth checks." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Child of Pollution (Su)]{l=4 icon=def-boost order=ps passive="A swarm monger gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against disease and poisons, and she can eat spoiled or rotting food and drink without ill effect." standard="A swarm monger can devour a handful of rotting food to gain a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to 1d8 + her druid level that last for 1 hour. She can use this ability a number of times a day equal to one-fourth of her druid level." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Swarm Shape (Su)]{l=12 icon=power ability="A swarm monger can use *wild shape* to transform into a swarm of vermin, functioning as ‹spell/swarm skin›, but not allowing her to split into more than one contiguous swarm.
