She doesn't leave her gear behind when she uses this ability, and she can revert to her mundane form normally." replace="The normal *wild shape* options gained at 12th level"}

tempest_druid.name: Tempest Druid
description: ## Tempest Druid
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/183;Inner Sea Magic/41›

Many of the druids who train under the aegis of the ‹rule/Storm Kindlers› focus on different areas of nature than those classically paid homage to by their kind. These so-called tempest druids see the personification of nature in the fury of the storm alone and to some extent eschew the veneration of plant or animal life, and the nature of their powers shifts accordingly. They still understand the place of plants and animals in the natural order, however; they simply view such entities - and indeed, all forms of life - as subservient to the true power found in the storms that lash the world.
::prereq{other="The beliefs held by tempest druids are inherently more chaotic than those held by most druids, and as a result, tempest druids must be chaotic neutral."}

::ab[Armor and Weapon Proficiencies]{icon=power ability="A tempest druid is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/trident›, in addition to the druid's normal armor and weapon proficiencies." alter="Armor and weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Spontaneous Domain Casting]{icon=magic ability="A tempest druid can channel stored spell energy into domain spells that he has not prepared ahead of time. He can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell to cast any domain spell of the same level or lower." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=down flavor="As a result of his beliefs, a tempest druid has little connection to the spirituality of animals or plants." choice="He can't choose an animal companion, and must instead select a domain or subdomain from the following list when he gains the *nature bond* ability: ‹domain/Air›, ‹druiddomain/Aquatic›, ‹domain/Cloud›, ‹domain/Storms›, ‹druiddomain/Swamp›, ‹domain/Weather›, or ‹domain/Wind›." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Sodden Shore Sense (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A tempest druid gains a +4 bonus on Knowledge (nature) and Survival checks in coastal or marshy lands." replace="Nature sense"}

::ab[Electrical Resistance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def order=ps passive="A tempest druid gains electricity resistance 5." standard="He can transfer this resistance to another creature for 1 hour, after which time it reverts to him." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Eyes of the Storm (Ex)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A tempest druid can see through 10 feet of magical fog, mist, rain, wind, or similar inclement weather conditions, ignoring any concealment such conditions might grant." imp8="His sight increases to 15 feet." imp12="His sight increases to 20 feet." imp16="His sight increases to 25 feet." imp20="His sight increases to 30 feet." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Bend Bolt (Su)]{l=9 icon=power flavor="A tempest druid can redirect nearby electrical attacks." immediate="The druid can shift the area or target of an electricity attack by 5 feet in any direction. If the electricity affects an area, the druid selects one square to be unaffected and an adjacent square to be affected. If the adjacent square is already in the area, this has no additional effect in that square. If the electricity affects a target, the druid selects an adjacent target. For example, if he were in the line of a wizard's lightning bolt, he could have the spell skip his square and instead affect an adjacent square, even if this meant the spell did not form a continuous line. If another druid attacked him with ‹spell/call lightning›, he could shift the called bolt to an adjacent square, affecting a creature in that square (if any)." special="He can't redirect electricity damage conducted to him by physical contact with an object or creature (such as a ‹spell/shocking grasp› or a ‹magic-enh/shock› weapon)." useMod=Wis replace="Venom immunity"}

tempest_tamer.name: Tempest Tamer
description: ## Tempest Tamer
‹SOURCE Blood of the Sea/25›

Druids draw upon the fiercest powers of nature, and tsunamis, typhoons, and whirlpools are among the most powerful forces in the ocean. Tempest tamers are at one with the water and gain the ability to soothe these dangerous phenomena. Tempest tamers can be found in many of Golarion's oceans, but all avoid the spectacular Eye of Abendego - they are all keenly aware of the supernatural origin of that massive hurricane, and they know they cannot quell its fury without first acquiring extraordinary power.

::ab[Speech of the Sea]{icon=power-down note="A tempest tamer's bonus language options include Aquan and Auran, in addition to the bonus languages available to the character from her race. However, the tempest tamer does not gain Sylvan as a bonus language option." alter="Bonus languages"}

::ab[Whirlpool Walker (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def flavor="A tempest tamer can resist winds and the unnatural powers of sea creatures." passive="The tempest tamer gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against spells with the air or water descriptor and the spell-like and supernatural abilities of creatures with the air, aquatic, or water subtype. This bonus also applies to saving throws to avoid damage from a vortex, whirlpool, or similar effects, as well as to saving throws to prevent being pulled into or moved by such effects." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Tempest Wild Shape (Su)]{l=4 icon=magic ability="A tempest tamer gains the ability to turn herself into a Small water elemental. This ability functions as per the ‹spell/elemental body I› spell except as noted here. The effect lasts for 1 hour per druid level or until she changes back. Changing form (to an elemental or back) is a @HLstandard_action and doesn't provoke attacks of opportunity." imp6="A tempest tamer can use *tempest wild shape* to change into a Small air elemental." imp8="A tempest tamer can use *tempest wild shape* to change into a Medium air or water elemental, as per ‹spell/elemental body II›." imp10="A tempest tamer can use *tempest wild shape* to change into a Large air or water elemental, as per ‹spell/elemental body III›." imp12="A tempest tamer can use *tempest wild shape* to change into a Huge air or water elemental, as per ‹spell/elemental body IV›." useInc="2~druid~4" imp20="She can use *tempest wild shape* at will." replace="Wild shape"}

toxicologist.name: Toxicologist
description: ## Toxicologist
‹SOURCE Magic Tactics Toolbox/23›

Druids protect the natural environs of the world, and sometimes that requires forcibly expelling those who would pollute, despoil, or otherwise harm such areas. Single battles may not be enough to win the war, so some druids specialize in the stealthy application of insidious poisons to their targets. In the verdant depths of the Mwangi Expanse or the Valashmai Jungle of Tian Xia, many druids work to root out their enemies by means of powerful toxins, believing word of their foes' horrifying deaths will deter others from attempting similar desecrations of the natural world.

::ab[Expanded Repertoire (Ex)]{icon=down-learn note="A toxicologist adds the following extracts and spells to her druid spell list as druid spells of the listed spell level." s1="Poisoned egg" s2="Transmute potion to poison" s3="Stinking cloud" s4="Toxic gift" s5=Cloudkill ability="A toxicologist can channel stored spell energy only into summoning spells she has prepared ahead of time." order=an alter=Spells replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Natural Poison Lore (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="A toxicologist has a deep understanding of and appreciation for poisons." l=2 ability="She cannot accidentally poison herself when applying poison to a weapon and cannot accidentally poison an animal companion or summoned creature when applying poison to its claws (though poison obviously should not be applied to a creature's bite)." replace="Wild empathy~woodland stride"}
::ab[Expert Poisoner (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost passive="A toxicologist increases the DCs of any spells she casts with the poison descriptor by 1." replace="Trackless step~resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Toxic Shaper (Su)]{icon=down l=4 ability="A toxicologist never gains the ability to use *wild shape* to transform into an elemental or plant." imp8="A toxicologist can transform into a Small or Medium vermin. When she takes this form, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/vermin shape I›." imp10="A toxicologist can transform into a tiny or large vermin. When she takes this form, her *wild shape* functions as ‹spell/vermin shape II›." alter="Wild shape"}

treesinger.name: Treesinger
description: ## Treesinger
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/25›

Elves live far longer than other common races, and a single elf may see whole empires rise and fall. Given the impermanence of the cultures around them, it's small wonder that some elves turn to the timeless growth of nature for solace, finding allies among the great trees themselves, and even leading the forest's plants into combat. A treesinger has the following class features.
::prereq{other="‹race/Elf› or ‹race/Vine Leshy›"}
::ab[Plant Bond (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power head choice="A treesinger forms a mystic bond with plant life. This bond can take one of two forms." replace="Nature bond"}

::ab[Domain]{sub icon=learn-magic ability="This grants the treesinger one of the following domains: ‹domain/Plant›, ‹domain/Growth›, ‹druiddomain/Jungle›, ‹druiddomain/Swamp›. When determining the powers and bonus spells granted by this domain, the treesinger's effective cleric level is equal to her druid level. A treesinger who selects this option also receives additional domain spell slots, just like a cleric. She must prepare the spell from her domain in this slot, and this spell cannot be used to cast a spell spontaneously."}

::ab[Plant companion]{sub icon=power ability="A treesinger can form a close bond with a plant companion. A treesinger may begin play with any of the plants listed below in @LINKPlant_Companions. This plant is a loyal companion that accompanies the treesinger on her adventures. Except for the companion being a creature of the plant type, drawn from the list of plant companions, this ability otherwise works like the standard druid's animal companion ability."}
:::ab{title="Green Empathy (Ex)" l=1 icon=power action=Ability replace="Wild empathy"}}

A treesinger can improve the attitude of a plant creature. This ability functions just like a Diplomacy check made to improve the attitude of a person. The treesinger rolls 1d20 and adds her druid level and her Charisma modifier to determine the *green empathy* check result. The typical wild plant creature has a starting attitude of indifferent.

To use *green empathy,* the treesinger and the plant creature must be within 30 feet of one another under normal conditions. Generally, influencing a plant creature in this way takes 1 minute but, as with influencing people, it might take more or less time.
A treesinger can also use this ability to influence an animal, but she takes a -4 penalty on the check.

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=4 icon=down-boost ability="A treesinger gains the ability to *wild shape.* This ability functions at her actual druid level. A treesinger cannot use *wild shape* to adopt an animal or elemental form. Instead, she can assume the form of a Small or Medium plant. This functions as ‹spell/plant shape I›, except the treesinger does not yet gain access to the constrict or poison abilities of the plant form assumed." imp8="The treesinger's *wild shape* gains the full range of abilities available from plant shape I." imp10="A treesinger can assume the form of a Large or Tiny plant. Her *wild shape* ability now functions like ‹spell/plant shape II›." imp12="A treesinger can assume the form of a Huge plant. Her *wild shape* ability now functions like ‹spell/plant shape III›." special="This ability otherwise functions like the normal druid *wild shape* ability." replace="Wild shape"}
::h3[Plant Companions]{anchor}

Each plant companion has different starting sizes, speed, attacks, ability scores, and special qualities. All plant attacks are made using the creature's full base attack bonus unless otherwise noted. Plant attacks add the plant's Strength modifier on the damage roll, unless it has only one attack, in which case it adds 1-1/2 times its Strength modifier. Some plant companions have special abilities, such as ‹umr/scent›. Plant companions cannot gain armor or weapon proficiency feats, even as they advance in hit dice, and cannot use manufactured weapons at all unless their description says otherwise.

As you gain levels, your plant companion grows in power as well. It gains the same bonuses that are gained by animal companions. Each plant companion gains an additional bonus, usually at 4th or 7th level, as listed with each plant choice. Instead of taking the listed benefit at 4th level, you can instead choose to increase the companion's Strength and Constitution by 2.
#### Original Plant Companions
The treesinger was introduced with four plant companions. No information was given about them other than their stat blocks.
- ‹companion/Carnivorous Flower›
- ‹companion/Crawling Vine›
- ‹companion/Puffball« (Floating Fungus)›
- ‹companion/Sapling Treant›
#### Later Plant Companions
Later books introduced other plant companions, specifically noting that they can be used by the treesinger and similar archetypes.
- ‹companion/Corpse-Eater Fungus› - This pale mass of fungal matter looks something like a spider with a thorny, acidic mouth in place of a body.

- ‹companion/Creeping Puffball› - This surprisingly mobile, multicolored ball of fungus crawls around on a nest of dozens of little filament-like "legs." Its poisonous spores cause those affected to endure bright, dazzling spots of color.

- ‹companion/Gulper Plant› - This bulbous green plant can climb or crawl via a network of vines. Its central reservoir can gulp down foes to digest them in an internal well of acid, holding them tight while they struggle.
- ‹companion/Hunting Cactus› - Able to crawl swiftly on five root-like legs, the hunting cactus has long arms it can slam foes with - arms covered with hundreds of thin, sharp needles.
- ‹companion/Rash Creeper› - This mass of creeping vines and leaves slithers almost like a snake. Contact with this predatory plant causes a sickening and sometimes debilitating rash.
- ‹companion/Slithering Sundew› - Looking something like a two-headed serpent, this slithering plant strikes with two sticky, constricting slams.
- ‹companion/Snapping Flytrap› - This mobile flytrap features two spiky jaws and scuttles about on a tangle of roots.

- ‹companion/Sniper Cactus› - This single-stalked cactus sports dozens of long, thin needles. Capable of crawling on three stalk-like roots, the sniper cactus can fire its thorns with deadly accuracy.

troll_fury.name: Troll Fury
description: ## Troll Fury
‹SOURCE Monster Codex/224›

Troll furies combine a druid's love of natural balance with a single-minded devotion to the welfare of the ‹family/troll› tribe. Trolls require a lot of prey to keep fed, and if their hunting grounds are upset by incursions of other monsters, civilized deforestation, or careless trolls who eat more than they need, a troll tribe can find itself without a sustainable food source.

Troll furies take the long view on sustainability, protecting the trolls' territory from gluttonous trolls and outsiders alike, and even hunting down tribal enemies when necessary. Holding themselves aloof from the rest of troll society, troll furies command great respect and can rally a region's trolls quickly to deal with threats to their existence and the health of their environment.
::prereq{other="‹monster/Troll›"}

::ab[Inspire Fervor (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def standard="Once per day, a troll fury can chant over another troll within 30 feet, bolstering it against fear and improving its combat abilities. This works like the ‹performance/inspire courage› bard ability, but affects only one troll and lasts 1 minute. The troll fury uses her druid level as her bard level to determine the effects of this ability." replace="Wild empathy"}

::ab[Chosen Prey (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost order=ca choice="A troll fury can select one creature type from the ranger's ‹ability/favored enemy› list and gains a favored enemy bonus against that creature type with an effective ranger level equal to her druid level. This ability never grants the troll fury favored enemy bonuses against a second favored enemy creature type." ability="A troll fury can change her favored enemy type once per week by performing a ritual that takes 1 hour to perform." special="If a troll fury already has a favored enemy from another class, the levels from the classes that grant favored enemies stack with this one to determine the favored enemy bonuses, and in this case she can gain other favored enemy creature types." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Troll Fury's Mark (Su)]{l=9 icon=boost passive="Each time a troll fury or her animal companion damages a creature with a natural attack, the troll fury gains a +10 insight bonus on Survival checks to track that creature for 24 hours. The duration of this ability does not stack with itself. Each time the troll fury or her animal companion hits the target, this ability lasts for the next 24 hours." replace="Venom immunity"}

undine_adept.name: Undine Adept
description: ## Undine Adept
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/176›

An undine adept dedicates herself to preserving the knowledge of the first undines and ensuring her people's ancient connections to the natural world remain undisturbed. They serve as the keepers of the roots of the undine people and as their protectors. An undine adept has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Undine}
::ab[Domains]{icon=info choice="An undine adept who chooses a domain must choose the ‹domain/Community› or ‹domain/Water› domain, or any subdomain of those domains."}

::ab[Amphibious (Su)]{l=2 icon=power ability="The undine adept gains the ‹subtype/aquatic› subtype and the ‹umr/amphibious› universal monster ability, allowing her to breathe water or air." replace="Woodland stride"}

::ab[Augment Summoning (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="Any creature with the water subtype the undine adept summons with either ‹spell/summon monster› or ‹spell/summon nature's ally› gains the benefits of the ‹feat/Augment Summoning› feat." replace="Trackless step"}

::ab[Resist Water's Call (Su)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An undine adept gains a +4 bonus on saving throws against the spell-like and supernatural abilities of outsiders with the aquatic or water subtype, fey with the aquatic or water subtype, and spells and effects with the water descriptor." replace="Resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=power-boost ability="An undine adept gains the ability to use *wild shape.* When an undine takes the form of a creature with the aquatic or water subtype, this ability functions at her class level + 1. For all other forms, her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her actual undine adept level. This ability otherwise functions as *wild shape.*" replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Commune with Water Spirits]{l=9 icon=learn ability="An undine adept adds ‹spell/commune› to her druid spell list. She may use this spell whether she worships a deity or elemental forces." replace="Venom immunity"}

urban_druid.name: Urban Druid
description: ## Urban Druid
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/101›

While many druids keep to the wilderness, some make their way within settlements, communing with the animals and vermin who live there and speaking for the nature that runs rampant in civilization's very cradle.

::ab[Spontaneous Casting]{icon=power ability="An urban druid can channel stored spell energy into domain spells that she has not prepared ahead of time. She can &quot&lose&quot& a prepared spell in order to cast any domain spell of the same level or lower." replace="Spontaneous casting"}

::ab[Nature Bond (Ex)]{icon=down-boost choice="An urban druid may not select an animal companion. Instead, she must choose from the following domains, rather than those usually available to druids: ‹domain/Charm›, ‹domain/Community›, ‹domain/Knowledge›, ‹domain/Nobility›, ‹domain/Protection›, ‹domain/Repose›, ‹domain/Rune›, or ‹domain/Weather›."}

::ab[Lorekeeper (Ex)]{l=2 icon=skill-boost passive="An urban druid adds ‹skill/Diplomacy› (CHA), ‹skill/Knowledge (history)› (INT), ‹skill/Knowledge (local)› (INT), and ‹skill/Knowledge (nobility)› (INT) skills to her list of class skills. She also receives a +2 bonus on these skill checks." replace="Woodland stride~trackless step"}
::ab[Resist Temptation (Ex)]{l=4 icon=def passive="An urban druid gains a +2 bonus on saves vs. divinations and enchantments." replace="Resist nature's lure"}
::ab[A Thousand Faces (Su)]{l=6 icon=boost ability="An urban druid gains this ability now instead of at 13th level."}
::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=8 icon=down ability="An urban druid gains *wild shape,* except that her effective druid level for the ability is equal to her druid level - 4."}
::ab[Mental Strength (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="An urban druid gains immunity to charm and compulsion effects." replace="Venom immunity"}

urushiol.name: Urushiol
description: ## Urushiol
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/27›
When an urushiol druid forms a bond with nature, it takes a vastly different form than for most druids. His body becomes increasingly toxic, allowing him to secrete a deadly poison through his pores.

:::ab{l=1 title="Toxic Secretions (Su)" icon=power action=Ability imp1="An urushiol can secrete this poison as a numbing injury poison on a natural weapon, unarmed strike, or manufactured weapon by spending 1 of his daily doses as a @HLstandard_action. He must choose a natural weapon, unarmed strike, or manufactured weapon in his hand that deals piercing or slashing damage and that is not already poisoned or poisonous. The weapon remains poisoned until the urushiol scores a hit with it, the weapon leaves his possession for more than 1 round, or the poison is wiped away." imp3="He can now create two doses of poison each day." imp4="An urushiol's body becomes so saturated with poison that even his flesh is poisonous. Whenever a creature damages the urushiol with a bite or ‹umr/swallow whole› attack, the urushiol can release ingested poison into the creature by spending 1 of his daily doses as an @HLimmediate_action." imp5="He can now produce three doses of poison per day." imp7="The urushiol can now produce four doses of poison per day. He can spend 2 daily doses of his poison as a @HLstandard_action to secrete a contact poison and coat one of his limbs with the secreted poison. The urushiol can deliver the poison by making a successful touch attack as a @HLstandard_action, which can be part of the same standard action he used to secrete the poison. If the attack misses, the poison persists upon his limb throughout the subsequent rounds until he hits a target or touches something else with his poisoned limb." imp9="He can now produce five doses of poison each day." imp11="The urushiol can produce six doses of poison each day. He can spend 3 daily doses of his poison as a @HLstandard_action to release a 20-foot-radius cloud of inhaled poison centered around him that lasts for 1 round." imp13="He can now produce seven doses of poison each day." imp14="An urushiol's venom changes in frequency to **1/round for 4 rounds.** In addition, when using his urushiol venom, an urushiol can spend an extra dose of his poison to change the damage type of the poison to **Dexterity damage** or **Constitution damage.** He can use this alteration in combination with any of the other abilities. For example, he could spend a total of 3 doses to create a cloud of inhaled poison that deals Constitution damage." imp15="He can produce eight doses of poison each day." imp17="He can now produce nine doses of poison daily." imp19="The urushiol can produce ten doses of poison each day." special="The urushiol's daily doses are replenished when he regains his spells, but any of his weapons that are still poisoned with urushiol venom count against his daily limit." replace="Nature bond~resist nature's lure"}

An urushiol's body becomes capable of producing a single dose of poison per day. The urushiol is immune to his own poison and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying his own poison or attacking with a weapon coated with it. Regardless of the delivery method he chooses, his poison has the following base statistics.
::aff[Urushiol Venom]{iconP poison dcF dcLev=urushiol dcMod=Wis dcIt=his freqR=2 effStr="1d3" cure1}
::ab[Venom Immunity (Ex)]{icon=boost l=4 ability="An urushiol gains *venom immunity* now, instead of at 9th level." alter="Venom immunity"}

wild_whisperer.name: Wild Whisperer
description: ## Wild Whisperer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/92›

A wild whisperer is an expert at studying, predicting, and explaining animal behavior. She is less interested in plants, fey, and other aspects of the natural world, and uses her gifts to tame or relocate dangerous beasts and soothe the hurts of wounded and sickly creatures.

::ab[Inspiration (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A wild whisperer gains an inspiration pool as the ‹class/investigator› class ability. A wild whisperer uses her druid level as her investigator level when determining the effects of this ability. Instead of free uses of inspiration on Knowledge, Linguistics, or Spellcraft skill checks, the wild whisperer can use inspiration on Handle Animal, Heal, Knowledge (geography), Knowledge (nature), Ride, Sense Motive, and Survival skill checks without expending a use of inspiration, provided she's trained in the relevant skill. She can also use inspiration on any *wild empathy* check without expending a use of inspiration. A wild whisperer's inspiration pool refreshes each day when she prepares spells." replace="Woodland stride~trackless step~resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Wild Shape (Ex)]{l=4 icon=down ability="A wild whisperer gains the *wild shape* ability, but she never gains access to any forms beyond Small and Medium animal forms, as ‹spell/beast shape I›." alter="Wild shape"}

::ab[Natural Expertise (Ex)]{l=6 icon=boost passive="A wild whisperer's powers of observation give her an advantage when she's fighting natural creatures. When using inspiration on an attack roll against an animal or a vermin or on a saving throw against an effect from an animal or a vermin, a wild whisperer has to expend only one use of inspiration instead of two." replace="The additional use of *wild shape* at 6th level"}

::ab[Investigator Talent]{l=8 icon=power choice="Wild whisperer selects an ‹ability/investigator talent<s›." replace="The additional use of *wild shape* at 8th level" special="The following investigator talents complement the wild whisperer archetype: ‹invtalent/amazing inspiration›, ‹invtalent/effortless aid›, ‹invtalent/eidetic recollection›, ‹invtalent/empathy›, ‹invtalent/inspirational expertise›, ‹invtalent/inspired alertness›, ‹invtalent/inspired intimidator›, ‹invtalent/perceptive tracking›, and ‹invtalent/tenacious inspiration›."}

wolf_shaman.name: Wolf Shaman
description: ## Wolf Shaman
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/103›
A shaman with this totem calls upon the clever wolf, capable of roaming alone yet wise enough to run with a pack when facing dangers too great for one alone.

::ab[Nature Bond]{icon=info choice="A wolf shaman who chooses an animal companion must select a ‹companion/wolf›. If choosing a domain, the wolf shaman must choose from the ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Community›, ‹domain/Liberation›, and ‹domain/Travel› domains." alter="Nature bond"}
::ab[Wild Empathy (Ex)]{icon=boost ability="A wolf shaman can use *wild empathy* with canines as a @HLfull-round_action with a +4 bonus." alter="Wild empathy"}

:::ab{title="Totem Transformation (Su)" icon=boost-magic l=2 action="Standard Action" imp7="This becomes a @HLmove_action." imp12="This becomes a @HLswift_action." useL=druid useUnit=minute useNC=1 special="This is a polymorph effect and cannot be used while the druid is using another polymorph effect, such as *wild shape.*" replace="Woodland stride"}
A wolf shaman may adopt an aspect of the wolf while retaining her normal form. The druid may select from the following bonuses:
- **Movement** (+20 enhancement bonus to land speed)
- **Senses** (low-light vision, ‹umr/scent›, +4 racial bonus to Survival when tracking by scent)
- **‹umr/Natural weapons›** (bite [1d4 plus trip] for a Medium druid, +2 CMB to trip)
While using *totem transformation,* the wolf shaman may speak normally and can cast ‹spell/speak with animals› (canines only) at will.

::ab[Totemic Summons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost-magic standard="A wolf shaman may cast ‹spell/summon nature's ally› to summon canines, and these summoned creatures gain ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to her druid level. She can apply the ‹template/young› template to any canine to reduce the level of the summoning spell required by one. She can also increase the level of summoning required by one in order to apply either the ‹template/advanced› or the ‹template/giant› template, or increase it by two to apply both the advanced and giant templates."}

::ab[Wild Shape (Su)]{l=6 icon=down-boost passive="A wolf shaman gains *wild shape,* but the ability functions at her druid level - 2. If she takes on the form of a canine, she instead uses her druid level + 2." replace="Wild shape"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=9 icon=power ability="A wolf shaman gains one of the following bonus feats: ‹feat/Greater Trip›, ‹feat/Improved Trip›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Skill Focus› (Stealth), or ‹feat/Spring Attack›." imp13="The druid gains another feat from the list above."  imp17="The druid gains a third bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites for these bonus feats." replace="Venom immunity"}

world_walker.name: World Walker
description: ## World Walker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/43›

While all druids traverse the wilderness with ease, the world walkers take it upon themselves to travel the entire world. Some act as messengers and scouts for druidic circles, while others have a seemingly unquenchable wanderlust; each new land provides new mysteries to discover and new wisdom to be gained from mastering those mysteries.

::ab[Favored Terrain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="The world walker gains the ranger's ‹ability/favored terrain› ability. She treats her druid level as her ranger level for this ability. If she has levels in both classes, both class's levels stack for determining the effect of this ability." replace="Trackless step~resist nature's lure"}

::ab[Path of Trees (Su)]{l=9 icon=magic ability="Once a day, a wanderer can step within a tree and then teleport from that tree to another one in a manner similar to the ‹spell/tree stride› spell." imp12="She can do this twice a day." imp13="She can use this ability to teleport to any other tree of its type up to 100 miles away." imp15="She can do this three times a day." replace="Venom immunity~timeless body"}
